ingredients.name: Hearty Radish
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+4
ingredients.where: Common in West Necluda and around Hateno; in grass and on hillsides.

ingredients.name: Big Hearty Radish
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+5
ingredients.where: Rarer cousin of the Hearty Radish; West/East Necluda hills and Hyrule Field edges.

ingredients.name: Hearty Truffle
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+1
ingredients.where: Under trees and on rock ledges in forests (Hyrule Ridge, Faron, Eldin caves).

ingredients.name: Big Hearty Truffle
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+4
ingredients.where: Rare; usually inside caves and on shaded cliff ledges across Hyrule.

ingredients.name: Hearty Bass
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+2
ingredients.where: Swims in sky-island ponds (West Hebra, Tabantha, Necluda Sky Archipelagos) and some lakes.

ingredients.name: Hearty Salmon
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+4
ingredients.where: Cold waters: Lake Kilsie in the Hebra Mountains, and Hyrule Castle docks.

ingredients.name: Endura Carrot
ingredients.where: Rare; grows in East Necluda and Hyrule Ridge — look for the orange root with green tops.

ingredients.name: Endura Shroom
ingredients.where: Caves and dim forest floors in Faron and the Deku Tree / Korok Forest area.

ingredients.name: Stamella Shroom
ingredients.where: Very common; shaded forest floors across Hyrule (Hyrule Field, Necluda, Faron).

ingredients.name: Stambulb
ingredients.where: A glowing bulb that grows on the sky islands (Great Sky Island and most floating islands).

ingredients.name: Staminoka Bass
ingredients.where: Mountain lakes and ponds (Hyrule Ridge, Lanayru, the highlands).

ingredients.name: Courser Bee Honey
ingredients.where: Beehives hanging from trees in forests; shoot them down (Hyrule Field, Faron, Akkala).

ingredients.name: Spicy Pepper
ingredients.where: Very common in temperate grasslands and forests across Hyrule.

ingredients.name: Sunshroom
ingredients.where: Warm regions — Eldin foothills and Death Mountain's cooler edges, plus some caves.

ingredients.name: Warm Safflina
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands; a red-tinged herb on the sands.

ingredients.name: Sizzlefin Trout
ingredients.where: Cold-region waters — Hebra and Gerudo Highlands lakes and rivers.

ingredients.name: Hydromelon
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert oases and around Gerudo Town; a pale green melon.

ingredients.name: Chillshroom
ingredients.where: Cold regions — Hebra, Gerudo Highlands, Mount Lanayru slopes; also caves.

ingredients.name: Cool Safflina
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands; a blue-tinged herb on the sands.

ingredients.name: Chillfin Trout
ingredients.where: Hot-region waters — Eldin and Gerudo Desert pools; the cold-blue fish.

ingredients.name: Voltfruit
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert around oases and on cactus-like plants near Gerudo Town.

ingredients.name: Zapshroom
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert and Wasteland, and some Hyrule caves; a yellow-capped mushroom.

ingredients.name: Electric Safflina
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands; a yellow-tinged herb on the sands.

ingredients.name: Voltfin Trout
ingredients.where: Lakes and rivers in Lanayru and Faron; the electric-yellow fish.

ingredients.name: Mighty Bananas
ingredients.where: Faron jungle and other warm forests; grow in bunches on palms.

ingredients.name: Razorshroom
ingredients.where: Forests across Hyrule (Hyrule Ridge, Akkala, Faron) and some caves.

ingredients.name: Mighty Thistle
ingredients.where: Hyrule Field, Eldin and Akkala hillsides; a spiky purple-flowered weed.

ingredients.name: Mighty Carp
ingredients.where: Lakes and rivers across central Hyrule and Necluda.

ingredients.name: Mighty Porgy
ingredients.where: Coastal waters of Lurelin Village and the southern Necluda sea.

ingredients.name: Ironshroom
ingredients.where: Rocky areas and caves in Eldin, Akkala and Hyrule Ridge.

ingredients.name: Fortified Pumpkin
ingredients.where: Grown in fields near Kakariko Village and some stables; a knobbly green gourd.

ingredients.name: Armoranth
ingredients.where: Hyrule Ridge, Akkala Highlands and northeastern Hyrule grasslands; a tall red-tipped plant.

ingredients.name: Armored Carp
ingredients.where: Rivers and lakes around Zora's Domain (Lanayru) and central Hyrule.

ingredients.name: Armored Porgy
ingredients.where: Coastal waters near Lurelin Village and the southern Necluda sea.

ingredients.name: Fleet-Lotus Seeds
ingredients.where: Floating lotus plants in ponds and wetlands (Lanayru, Faron, Wetlands).

ingredients.name: Rushroom
ingredients.where: On cliff faces and rocky walls (Lanayru cliffs, Hyrule Ridge); a small green shroom.

ingredients.name: Swift Carrot
ingredients.where: Common in Necluda and near Kakariko/Hateno; the long pale root.

ingredients.name: Swift Violet
ingredients.where: Glowing purple flowers on shaded cliff ledges (Hyrule Ridge, Lanayru, Faron).

ingredients.name: Silent Shroom
ingredients.where: Shaded forest floors at night (Hyrule Field, Necluda, Faron); a glowing blue cap.

ingredients.name: Silent Princess
ingredients.where: Rare blue-white wildflower in remote meadows (Korok Forest fringe, Satori-area, Faron).

ingredients.name: Blue Nightshade
ingredients.where: Shaded forest patches and cliff bases (Hyrule Ridge, Lanayru, Faron).

ingredients.name: Stealthfin Trout
ingredients.where: Lakes and rivers at night (Lanayru, Faron); the fish that glows faintly blue.

ingredients.name: Brightcap
ingredients.where: Throughout the Depths; a luminous mushroom growing on roots and walls.

ingredients.name: Glowing Cave Fish
ingredients.where: Caves and well waters across the surface; the small fish that glows in the dark.

ingredients.name: Sun Pumpkin
ingredients.where: Grown at Kakariko Village's pumpkin patch and some Depths bloom spots; heals 1 gloom heart each.

ingredients.name: Sundelion
ingredients.where: Sky islands — a white bloom on the floating land; heals up to 3 gloom hearts each.

ingredients.name: Apple
ingredients.where: On and under trees almost everywhere in Hyrule — Hyrule Field, forests, around the surface in general.

ingredients.name: Palm Fruit
ingredients.where: On palm trees in warm/coastal areas — Lurelin Village, the Faron and Necluda coasts, Akkala beaches.

ingredients.name: Wildberry
ingredients.where: On bushes in cold, snowy regions — Hebra, Tabantha, the Gerudo Highlands.

ingredients.name: Hylian Tomato
ingredients.where: On bushes from cold to warm areas — around Hyrule Field and West Necluda; sold at general stores.

ingredients.name: Hyrule Herb
ingredients.where: Sprouts in grassy fields throughout Hyrule, often near Hyrule Field and the central plains.

ingredients.name: Hyrule Bass
ingredients.where: In ponds and rivers across Hyrule Field and West Necluda; easy to scoop in shallows.

ingredients.name: Sanke Carp
ingredients.where: In lakes and rivers, notably around Lake Hylia and the Lanayru wetlands; throw bait/fruit to lure them.

ingredients.name: Ancient Arowana
ingredients.where: In the rivers and waters of Tabantha and the Hebra region (cold north).

ingredients.name: Raw Meat
ingredients.where: Dropped by small animals — boars, mountain goats, foxes. Plentiful across the surface plains.

ingredients.name: Raw Prime Meat
ingredients.where: Dropped by medium-sized animals — buck deer, large boars, wolves, bears.

ingredients.name: Raw Gourmet Meat
ingredients.where: Dropped by large animals — Water Buffalo, Tabantha Moose, cows/bears (Hebra, Tabantha).

ingredients.name: Raw Bird Drumstick
ingredients.where: Dropped by small birds — pigeons, crows, ducks, herons, seagulls — when killed.

ingredients.name: Raw Bird Thigh
ingredients.where: Dropped by medium birds such as the Eldin Ostrich and Blue-Winged Heron.

ingredients.name: Raw Whole Bird
ingredients.where: Dropped by large birds — the Mountain Buzzard, Eldin Ostrich, White Pigeon and similar large fowl.

ingredients.name: Bird Egg
ingredients.where: In nests in trees, on cliffs, and around stables/villages; from chicken coops at Hateno and Lurelin.

ingredients.name: Fresh Milk
ingredients.where: Bought from general stores (Hateno, Kakariko, Rito) and from ranchers near stables.

ingredients.name: Goat Butter
ingredients.where: Bought from general stores (Hateno, Kakariko, Rito); used to enrich dishes, cakes and stews.

ingredients.name: Hylian Rice
ingredients.where: Cut from tall grass near Hateno and across Hyrule; sold at the Hateno General Store.

ingredients.name: Tabantha Wheat
ingredients.where: Cut from grass in the Tabantha Frontier; sold at Rito Village and Gerudo Town general stores.

ingredients.name: Cane Sugar
ingredients.where: Bought from general stores (Goron City, Gerudo Town, Rito); used to make sweets and cakes.

ingredients.name: Rock Salt
ingredients.where: Mined from rock-salt deposits in caves and on mountainsides (common in Eldin/Death Mountain caves).

ingredients.name: Acorn
ingredients.where: Found at the base of trees and dropped by squirrels in forests across Hyrule; very common.

ingredients.name: Hightail Lizard
ingredients.bonus: Speed Up (movement/run/swim/climb). Pair with a monster part to brew a Hasty Elixir.
ingredients.where: Common on grasslands across Hyrule Field, West Necluda and the Sahasra Slope; scurries fast, sneak or shoot it.

ingredients.name: Hot-Footed Frog
ingredients.bonus: Speed Up. Brews a Hasty Elixir with any monster part. 1-4 frogs + 1 monster part.
ingredients.where: Near water in Lanayru Great Spring and East Necluda; also sold by Beedle at some stables.

ingredients.name: Tireless Frog
ingredients.bonus: Enduring effect: overfills the stamina wheel with extra (yellow) stamina.
ingredients.where: Near ponds in Lanayru Great Spring and Hyrule Ridge during rain; in wells in eastern Hyrule; sold by Beedle at several stables.

ingredients.name: Energetic Rhino Beetle
ingredients.bonus: Energizing effect: instantly restores part of the stamina wheel. More potent than the Restless Cricket.
ingredients.where: On trees at night/early morning in Faron, Akkala Highlands (near South Akkala Stable) and West Necluda; also from Beedle at stables.

ingredients.name: Restless Cricket
ingredients.bonus: Energizing effect: restores stamina. Weaker than the Energetic Rhino Beetle (easier to catch, so less potent).
ingredients.where: Hops in grassy meadows of Hyrule Field on trees or in freshly cut grass; cut grass to flush them out.

ingredients.name: Warm Darner
ingredients.bonus: Spicy Elixir = COLD resistance (warms you). Interchangeable with the Summerwing Butterfly.
ingredients.where: Flies over warm grasslands in Hyrule Field, Faron and Necluda; near water and meadows. Also sold at Akkala/Hyrule Field stables.

ingredients.name: Summerwing Butterfly
ingredients.bonus: Spicy Elixir = COLD resistance (warms you). Interchangeable with the Warm Darner.
ingredients.where: Eldin Canyon, Gerudo Highlands, Tabantha Bridge Stable, Kara Kara Bazaar, Snowfield Stable; bought from Beedle for ~10 rupees.

ingredients.name: Cold Darner
ingredients.bonus: Chilly Elixir = HEAT resistance (cools you). Interchangeable with the Winterwing Butterfly.
ingredients.where: Flutters in cool, often forested or highland areas; common around Hyrule Ridge and northern fields.

ingredients.name: Winterwing Butterfly
ingredients.bonus: Chilly Elixir = HEAT resistance (cools you). Interchangeable with the Cold Darner.
ingredients.where: Cold regions: Hebra Mountains, Tabantha Frontier and the Gerudo Highlands snowfields.

ingredients.name: Electric Darner
ingredients.bonus: Electro Elixir = shock resistance. Interchangeable with the Thunderwing Butterfly.
ingredients.where: Near water and humid grasslands in Lanayru Great Spring and Faron; often by rivers.

ingredients.name: Thunderwing Butterfly
ingredients.bonus: Electro Elixir = shock resistance. Interchangeable with the Electric Darner.
ingredients.where: Gerudo Desert and Gerudo Highlands, especially where thunderstorms roll through.

ingredients.name: Fireproof Lizard
ingredients.bonus: Fireproof Elixir = flame guard (won't burn in extreme heat / Eldin). Pair with a monster part.
ingredients.where: Death Mountain, Eldin Canyon and Eldin Mountains; along the Goronbi River and in most Eldin caves.

ingredients.name: Smotherwing Butterfly
ingredients.bonus: Fireproof Elixir = flame guard. Interchangeable with the Fireproof Lizard.
ingredients.where: Death Mountain and Eldin Canyon, fluttering around the hottest volcanic areas.

ingredients.name: Bladed Rhino Beetle
ingredients.bonus: Mighty Elixir = Attack Up. With strong monster parts (e.g. Lynel Guts) reaches Mighty++.
ingredients.where: On trees in Hyrule Field and West Necluda, and around Faron's woods; shake or hit the tree to drop it.

ingredients.name: Rugged Rhino Beetle
ingredients.bonus: Tough Elixir = Defense Up. With strong monster parts (e.g. Hinox Guts) reaches Tough++.
ingredients.where: On trees in Eldin Canyon and the Akkala Highlands; found on tree trunks in warmer wooded areas.

ingredients.name: Sunset Firefly
ingredients.bonus: Sneaky Elixir = Stealth Up (quieter, enemies notice you less). Also used to make Sneaky dishes.
ingredients.where: Appears at night in grassy fields and forests (Hyrule Field, Necluda); crouch and approach slowly or it flees.

ingredients.name: Deep Firefly
ingredients.bonus: Bright Elixir = Glow (Link emanates light in dark caves / the Depths). Striking it destroys it - sneak-catch only.
ingredients.where: Caves and the Depths, especially around the Great Hyrule Forest and underground hollows; glows in the dark.

ingredients.name: Hearty Lizard
ingredients.bonus: hearty:+4 - Hearty Elixir = full heal + temporary (yellow) hearts; no timer, the extra hearts persist until lost. One lizard grants ~4 bonus hearts.
ingredients.where: Gerudo Highlands and East Necluda; clings to logs and rocks. Beedle sells them at some stables for ~100 rupees.

ingredients.name: Sticky Lizard
ingredients.bonus: Sticky Elixir = SLIP RESISTANCE (climb in rain without sliding). NOTE: Slip Resistance is not one of the simulator's modeled effects, so the pot tool won't predict a tier for it - it's informational here.
ingredients.where: Clings to cave walls and rock faces throughout Hyrule, often near water in Lanayru and the Zora region.

ingredients.name: Sticky Frog
ingredients.bonus: Sticky Elixir = SLIP RESISTANCE (climb in rain). Interchangeable with the Sticky Lizard. NOTE: not one of the simulator's modeled effects.
ingredients.where: Poolside after rainfall and in caves near water in West Necluda and Lanayru Great Spring; on wet rock.

ingredients.name: Fairy
ingredients.bonus: Carry one and it AUTO-REVIVES Link on death (consumes the fairy). Restores 5 hearts if used raw; cook with a monster part for a Fairy Tonic (no stat effect, just healing). Sneak-catch or grab while gliding - it flees once it notices you.
ingredients.where: Floats near circular ponds on the Sky Islands and in some surface caves; easiest to farm from Great Fairy Fountains' sky-island ponds.

ingredients.name: Bokoblin Horn
ingredients.where: Dropped by Bokoblins everywhere across Hyrule's surface, Sky, and Depths. The most common monster part.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. No effect of its own — pair with a critter to set the elixir's effect.

ingredients.name: Bokoblin Fang
ingredients.where: Dropped by Blue and Black Bokoblins (tougher color tiers). Common once you reach mid-game enemy camps.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration — over twice a horn's. A reliable mid-tier duration part.

ingredients.name: Bokoblin Guts
ingredients.where: Dropped by Silver Bokoblins (and rarely Black). Late-game, high-tier enemies.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration and a 30% critical-cook chance. A long-duration, high-value part.

ingredients.name: Moblin Horn
ingredients.where: Dropped by Moblins on the surface, in the Sky, and in the Depths.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. Functions like a Bokoblin Horn — short-duration base part.

ingredients.name: Moblin Fang
ingredients.where: Dropped by Blue and Black Moblins. Found at larger monster camps.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration — a solid mid-tier choice.

ingredients.name: Moblin Guts
ingredients.where: Dropped by Silver Moblins (rarely Black). Late-game enemies.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration plus a 30% crit-cook chance. High-tier, long-lasting.

ingredients.name: Lizalfos Horn
ingredients.where: Dropped by Lizalfos in wetlands, deserts, the Sky, and the Depths.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. A cheap, very common base part.

ingredients.name: Lizalfos Talon
ingredients.where: Dropped by Blue, Black, and elemental Lizalfos.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration. The Lizalfos mid-tier duration part.

ingredients.name: Lizalfos Tail
ingredients.where: Dropped by Lizalfos (rare ~25% drop), and from tougher color tiers. Farm in the Lanayru Wetlands.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration and a 30% critical-cook chance — high-tier, easy to farm.

ingredients.name: Lynel Saber Horn
ingredients.where: Dropped by Lynels (Coliseum, Hebra, the Depths). Every Lynel drops one when defeated.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration, but worth a lot when sold (40 rupees; more for higher-maned tiers).

ingredients.name: Lynel Mace Horn
ingredients.where: Dropped by Lynels alongside the Saber Horn. Both horn types drop from a single Lynel kill.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. Like the Saber Horn, it's better sold (40 rupees) than cooked.

ingredients.name: Lynel Guts
ingredients.where: Dropped only by Lynels — one of the rarest, most valuable monster parts in the game.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration plus a 30% crit-cook chance, and sells for 200 rupees. Best when sold, not cooked.

ingredients.name: Hinox Tooth
ingredients.where: Dropped by Hinox (surface, Sky, and Depths). Knock one out and loot its body.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +1:50 to elixir duration — a strong mid-tier part.

ingredients.name: Hinox Guts
ingredients.where: Dropped by tougher Hinox (Blue, Black). One of the better long-duration parts.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +2:40 to elixir duration and a 30% crit-cook chance. High value, long-lasting.

ingredients.name: Keese Eyeball
ingredients.where: Dropped by Keese (and elemental Keese — Fire/Ice/Electric). Abundant in caves and at night.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +1:20 to elixir duration. Cheap and plentiful — good filler when you don't need long duration.

ingredients.name: Chuchu Jelly
ingredients.where: Dropped by Chuchus everywhere. The most abundant monster ingredient in the game.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +0:40 to elixir duration. Endless supply makes it the go-to throwaway duration part.

ingredients.name: Aerocuda Eyeball
ingredients.where: Dropped by Aerocudas — the flying eye-creatures common in the Sky and over surface ruins.
ingredients.bonus: Adds +1:20 to elixir duration. A common low-tier part, slightly better duration than a horn.

ingredients.name: Dinraal's Scale
ingredients.where: Shoot Dinraal's body (torso/tail). Dinraal, the fire dragon, circles the northern sky — rises from the East Akkala Plains Chasm / Eldin region and loops the Eldin Mountains, Akkala, and Tabantha sky. One part drops per real-world ~10 min, then its body glows again.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (Carries no effect of its own — it boosts whatever critter you cook it with. On weapon fusion it also adds a minor fire effect.)

ingredients.name: Shard of Dinraal's Horn
ingredients.where: Shoot the tip of Dinraal's horn — the rarest, hardest part to hit. Dinraal rises from Eldin/Akkala and flies the northern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the longest cooking duration in the game. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Naydra's Scale
ingredients.where: Shoot Naydra's body (torso/tail). Naydra, the ice dragon, rises from the Naydra Snowfield Chasm by the Mount Lanayru Skyview Tower and circles the Lanayru / eastern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (No effect of its own. On weapon fusion it also adds a minor ice effect.)

ingredients.name: Shard of Naydra's Horn
ingredients.where: Shoot the tip of Naydra's horn (rare drop). Naydra flies the Lanayru / Mount Lanayru / eastern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the maximum cooking duration. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Farosh's Scale
ingredients.where: Shoot Farosh's body (torso/tail). Farosh, the lightning dragon, rises from the East Gerudo Chasm and circles the southwest — Gerudo Canyon, Faron Grasslands, and toward Lake Hylia. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (No effect of its own. On weapon fusion it also adds a minor shock effect.)

ingredients.name: Shard of Farosh's Horn
ingredients.where: Shoot the tip of Farosh's horn (rare drop). Farosh flies the Gerudo / Faron / Lake Hylia southwestern sky. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the maximum cooking duration. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Light Dragon's Scale
ingredients.where: Shoot the Light Dragon's body (torso/tail). The Light Dragon flies one wide fixed loop around all of Hyrule and is the only dragon that never enters the Depths/chasms; reach it via the sky islands or a Skyview Tower launch. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and adds about 1:30 to the dish's effect duration. (No effect of its own. On weapon fusion Light Dragon parts add a healing/recovery effect instead of an element.)

ingredients.name: Shard of Light Dragon's Horn
ingredients.where: Shoot the tip of the Light Dragon's horn (rare drop). The Light Dragon flies a continuous loop across the whole map. One part per real-world ~10 min.
ingredients.bonus: Guarantees a Critical Success and maxes the dish to 30:00 — the maximum cooking duration. One horn shard takes any timed dish to ~30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Star Fragment
ingredients.where: Catch or pick up where a shooting star lands at night; one falls nightly and vanishes by ~5 AM.
ingredients.bonus: crit-chance

ingredients.name: Monster Extract
ingredients.where: Buy for 50 rupees at the Tarrey Town General Store (also stocked by some monster-camp/merchant pots).

ingredients.name: Dragon Horn Shard
ingredients.where: Shoot the horn tip of Dinraal, Naydra, Farosh, or the Light Dragon (one horn shard per dragon per Blood Moon).
ingredients.bonus: crit

ingredients.name: Dragon Scale
ingredients.where: Strike a dragon's body/tail (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light Dragon) to knock off a scale (~10-min part cooldown).
ingredients.bonus: crit

ingredients.name: Dragon Claw
ingredients.where: Hit a dragon's feet/claws (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light Dragon; ~10-min part cooldown).
ingredients.bonus: crit

ingredients.name: Dragon Fang
ingredients.where: Hit a dragon's mouth/jaw (Dinraal/Naydra/Farosh/Light Dragon; ~10-min part cooldown).
ingredients.bonus: crit

ingredients.name: Golden Apple
ingredients.where: Rare golden apples on apple trees around Hyrule (e.g. the orchard by Sonapan Shrine on Satori Mountain, Hyrule Field, Mido Swamp near the Lost Woods).
ingredients.bonus: crit
